title: APPEAR — Enemy-appear descent
tldr: LFSR-noise with a *descending* clock — `LFREQ` increases over the sound's lifetime, so the noise pitch *falls*. The "uh-oh, something just spawned" cue.
how: The LFSR generates the same broadband noise as LITE, but APPEAR's outer loop steers `LFREQ` upward (longer period between shifts → lower-frequency noise). Where LITE's pitch *rises* over time, APPEAR's *drops*. Same engine, opposite ramp direction.
watch: Spectrogram — descending broadband noise vs LITE's ascending.
`LFREQ` *increasing* over time (look at the Code panel's LFSR line).
code: `APPEAR` entry, then shared LFSR inner loop.
